sections.paragraph:
ἔμφοβος, ον (=‘terrible’ in Soph., Oed. C. 39) pert. to being in a state of fear, afraid, startled, terrified (Theophr., Char., 25, 1; Vett. Val. 59, 7; PGM 13, 873; Sb 7452, 13; Sir 19:24) ἔ. γίνεσθαι be afraid, etc. (cp. Aesop 49 P.=83 H.=H-H. 40 III) Lk 24:5, 37; Ac 10:4; 22:9 v.l.; 24:25 (s. Field, Notes 139); Rv 11:13.—DELG s.v. φέβομαι. M-M.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Field, Field, Notes = Notes on the Translation of the NT

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 326
